free_text: Measurement of neutron excitation functions using wide
energy neutron beams.
G.Loevestam, M.Hult, A.Fessler, T.Gamboni,
J.Gasparro, R.Jaime, P.Lindahl, S.Oberstedt.
H.Tagziria.
Technique disks of natural indium.Isototic abundances:
diameter, 5 mm thickness. Distance between the
neutron-producing target and disk was 20 cm.
IRMM Van de Graaff.
Laboratory stationary long counter -for neutron
beam monitoring.
IRMM Bonner sphere neutron spectrometer (PTB-C
design, MAXED unfolding program) for incident neutron
spectra measurement.
Low background HPGe-detector (Ge-T2) for gamma
ray measurement. Detectors is of 20.% relative
efficiency, coaxial detector with ultra-pure aluminum
(Kryal) end-cap and entrance window of 1.5mm thick.
Active volume of Ge-crystal 71.3 cm**3, crystal
diameter 59 mm. Calibrated experimentally by point
sources at different distances from detector,
average discrepancy of measured activity < 1.5 %.
H-3(p,n) to produce neutrons in 0.5-2.8 MeV
energy range. Ti-H-3 target of 2.075 mg/cm**2.
H-3 to Ti ratio was calculated to be 1.22 at the time
for irradiation. Neutron spectrum was measured at
deuteron energy of 2.90 MeV, giving a peak neutron
energy of 6.0 MeV.
Neutron spectrum was measured at proton energy of
3.32 MeV, giving a peak neutron energy of 2.5 MeV.
H-2(d,n) to produce neutrons in 4.0 - 5.5 MeV
energy range. Solid Ti-H-2 target of 2.065 mg/cm**2 .
H-2/Ti ratio was 1.48 .
The given neutron energies are for 0 degrees relative
the ion beam.Since the target(the disk) to Be activated
is scanned in front of the neutron producing target
the disk is irradiated by a continuum of neutron
energies from 0-5.5 MeV.
Simulated and measured spectra agree well in the higher
energy region, but considerable difference is noticed
in the lower energy part ( Fig.5 of NIM/A,580,1400).
10 irradiations:
En    F(0deg)  F(80deg) Irrad.time  A(In113) A(In115)
(MeV) (10**3n/cm**2/sec)  (sec)   (10**-21 Bq/atoms)
En - neutron peak energy,
F - average neutron fluence rate, calculated by
Target code, at distance of 20 cm from neutron-
producing target, i.e at the front side of sample,
A(In113) - measured activity of In-113-m,
A(In115) - measured activity of In-115-m
( with standard uncertainties, calculated following
ISO/BIPM Guide to Expression of Uncertainty in
Measurement, ISBN:92-67-10188-9).
Simulated total neutron fluence spectra are given on
Fig.3 of J,NIM/A,580,1400.
No corrections for multiple scattered neutrons in the
disk material .
No corrections for difference in solid angle due to
the finite thickness of the disks.
Uncertainty of geometrical characteristics
of sample disks  < 0.5 %.
Uncertainty of sample mass determination.
Uncertainty of counting statistics around or less 1%.
Uncertainty in detector efficiency estimation.
Uncertainty of default curve.
Uncertainty of calculations of neutron fluence
fields, based on measurement and calculations.
Sum of uncertainties, including the
measurement of distance between neutron-producing
target and samples,the weighting of samples, the
measurement of sample dimensions and the normalization
to ion beam current  up to 1.5%.
Total uncertainty.
The MAXED code comes in a software package called UMG
(Unfolding using MAXED and GRAVEL) which also
facilitates uncertainty calculations using output data
from the MAXED code (author's comment).
Request for the data was sent 07.09.2009 to
Goeran Loevestam. Data received from Goeran Loevestam
Goeran Loevestam, 14.09.2009.
M.M.
Nuclear data used.
Cross-section data were unfolded to 140 channels
covering the neutron energy region 0 - 5.6 MeV.
IRDF-2002 data were used as default curve in unfolding.
Normalization was done to the integrated cross-section,
sig-115def, calculated for up to 5.6 MeV neutrons to
1.380 b from default curve.
The MAXED unfolding routine allows for uncertainty
propagation.
Uncertainty of emission probability for
336 keV gamma line.
En,MeV    Total uncertainty, %
Data received from Goeran Loevestam 08.09.2009.
Data are presented on Fig. 6, bottom, of
NDS(VZ): REL-REF: ND/B->NDS
Nuclear data used.
Cross-section data were unfolded to 140 channels
covering the neutron energy region 0 - 5.6 MeV.
9 degrees polynomial fit to selected data points from
EXFOR were used as default curve in unfolding.
Normalization was done to the integrated cross-section,
sig-113, calculated to 1.196 b, using the integrated
cross section for In-115, sig-115def, and corresponding
integrated values for the unfolded excitation functions
for In-115 and In-113, f-113 and f-115,respectively:
The MAXED unfolding routine allows for uncertainty
propagation.
Data received from Goeran Loevestam 08.09.2009.
Data are presented on Fig. 6, top of J,NIM/A,580,1400.
